steps.title: Find the number of gaps between nails
steps.content:
The wall spans from the first nail at the left edge to the last nail at the right edge, a distance of $3x$ feet. Each consecutive nail is $\frac{3}{8}$ feet apart. The number of gaps between nails is:
$$\text{Number of gaps} = \frac{3x}{\frac{3}{8}} = 3x \cdot \frac{8}{3} = 8x$$

steps.title: Apply the fence post principle to count nails
steps.content:
When a length is divided into equal gaps from one end to the other, the number of boundary points (nails) is always one more than the number of gaps. With $8x$ gaps, the total number of nails is:
$$y = 8x + 1$$
This matches the correct answer — D is correct.
